title: Data centers could be 33% of commercial building electricity use by 2050: EIA
snippet: In one modeled scenario, the Energy Information Administration projects server electricity consumption in 2050 will be more than 16 times what it was in 2020.
ai_summary: Long-term forecast showing data center electricity demand growth in US commercial sector. Relevant as background on emerging I&C customer trends and demand patterns.
ai_reasoning: Data centers are a potential I&C customer segment, and understanding emerging demand trends is useful for business planning. However, this is a US-focused forecast with no direct impact on tem's current operations, pricing, or strategy this week.

title: Ofgem energy price cap sees inflation fall - but cost of living could spike in coming months - The Mirror
snippet: Ofgem energy price cap sees inflation fall - but cost of living could spike in coming months  The Mirror
ai_summary: Ofgem price cap changes affect the broader energy market context tem operates in, particularly for understanding wholesale price dynamics and customer acquisition timing in the I&C space.
ai_reasoning: While Ofgem price cap announcements are significant for the energy market, this is domestic consumer-focused news. tem operates in I&C, which is not subject to price caps. Relevant as background context on regulatory environment and market sentiment, but no direct business impact.
